claim: AWS Strands is an open-source, model-agnostic agent framework with built-in MCP and memory management, supporting both graph and swarm patterns
actionable: Use Strands for multi-agent orchestration to avoid lock-in; choose between graph (strict pipeline) and swarm (parallel exploration) based on use case

claim: Multi-agent workflows benefit from specialized agent roles with custom tools and prompts orchestrated together (research, analysis, synthesis pattern)
actionable: Design agent teams with specific roles (researcher, analyst, synthesizer); delegate tool access and prompts per role to improve accuracy

claim: Custom Python tools for agents work better than abstract APIs; write domain-specific scrapers, data loaders, and processors as agent tools
actionable: Build custom Python tool functions decorated with docstrings explaining behavior; agents reason better over concrete, domain-specific tools

claim: Shared memory between agents in multi-agent systems reduces redundant context passing and improves efficiency without manual state management
actionable: Use framework-provided shared memory (like Strands) to let agents read from previous agent outputs without explicit data passing
